title: Floral Crumbly Vietnamese Couscous Stir-fry
description: A diabetic-friendly sick-day dish featuring floral notes from spices and a crumbly texture of couscous that's perfect for quick meals.
instructions: Combine couscous with olive oil and a pinch of salt in a mixing bowl. Mix well until all grains are coated.
Spread the seasoned couscous evenly on a baking sheet lined with parchment paper. Bake for about 15 minutes or until golden brown, stirring occasionally.
While couscous is baking, heat remaining oil in a wok or large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic; sauté for about 2 minutes or until onions are translucent.
Add red bell pepper to the pan and stir-fry for an additional 5-7 minutes until peppers are slightly softened.
Stir the baked couscous into the wok with vegetables, adding more salt if needed. Cook for another minute.
Serve hot as a quick snack or side dish.
